results.title: Sheet steel for a nation
results.brief_description: Describes the process of cold reduction of sheet steel as a stage in the production of galvanised, corrugated and other types of steel sheets. This process was first intoroduced in 1955 at Port Kembla in New South Wales. Shows how in the major operation, the steel strip rolls through rollers which exert tremendous pressure in reducing the steel to the required thickness.

results.title: Sheet metal in industry
results.creator_credit: Bruce McNaughton and Academy Film Productions
results.brief_description: Sheet metal work in Australia, showing manufacture of varied articles including domestic hardware, surgical instruments, refrigerator cabinets, heating and ventilating ducts, vehicle bodies and jet engines. Shows the kinds of work available in the field

results.title: Sheepdog
results.creator_credit: John Alderson and British Sccreen Service
results.brief_description: The work of a typical English dog in the wild Welsh mountains. A study of a shepherd’s workmate, his sheepdog; used to protect flocks from marauding foxes, to round up stragglers, and to rescue injured lambs or sick ewes.

results.title: Sheep sheep sheep
results.brief_description: Illustrates the moods, rythms and images of sheep, using natrual sounds and no commentary. Shows sheep grazing, sleeping, waking and moving to the highlands.

results.title: Sheep ranching country: Southeastern Australia
results.brief_description: A study of sheep raising and wheat farming on the semi-arid plains region of South-Eastern Austalia. Emphasises the importance of wool and wheat exports to our economy.

results.title: Sheep in wood
results.brief_description: The film presents Jacques Hnizodvsky wroking in his studio on a woodcut of two rams locked in combat. Uses close-up photography to show each step in the process of making a woodcut, from preliminary setches to the artist’s proof. There is no narration.

results.title: Shearing technique
results.brief_description: A demonstration showing correct methods of shearing sheep. A shearer demonstrates the most common faults found in shearing and slow-motion “shots” help to emphasise the important points of good shearing.

results.title: Shaw versus Shakespeare. Part 1: the character of Caesar
results.creator_credit: John Barnes and Encyclopaedia Britannica Films
results.brief_description: George Bernard Shaw, played by Donald Moffatt, analyses Shakespear’s characterisation of Julis Caesar and compares it with his won treatment. Shaw claims the character of Caesar was beyind Shakespeare, but admits Shakespear is still the “King of the dramatists”.

results.title: Sharpening and tempering farm tools
results.brief_description: Explains how to treat carbon-steel tools for forge sharpening; how to sharpen, harden and temper a cultivator and a ploughshare shovel; how to identify tempering colours. From the series “Farm work forging”.

results.title: Sharkey's Party
results.brief_description: Sharky’s friends throw a wild party to celebrate his homecoming after six months of psychiatric treatment. This is a satirical look at friendship in the 1980s and the “sex and drugs and rock n roll” ethos.
